jokes.text: Go get yourself 10 sodas and you never saw us. Or just water. Just choose water.
jokes.setup: Gary bribes a kid to keep quiet about their meeting
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines his own bribe by suggesting water instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary character moment - shows his neurotic overthinking and health obsession even during covert operations

jokes.text: I don't even paint my own nails, Gary. A Vietnamese lady does that.
jokes.setup: Gary suggests they paint to blend in at art shop
jokes.punchline: Amy's privileged dismissal with casual racial specificity
jokes.explanation: Typical Amy - reveals her elitist bubble while being matter-of-fact about it

jokes.text: I am very good at my job, and even I can't get people to vote for that heap of mystery meat.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the difficult Families First Bill
jokes.punchline: 'Heap of mystery meat' as description of legislation
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor comparing bad legislation to unidentifiable cafeteria food

jokes.text: She has gone full Colonel Kurtz.
jokes.setup: Learning Selina wants her own bill to fail
jokes.punchline: Apocalypse Now reference - Selina as mad military commander
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference that captures Selina's descent into political madness

jokes.text: Is she giving orders from under the desk?
jokes.setup: Colonel Kurtz reference established
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo mixing presidential imagery with Apocalypse Now
jokes.explanation: Dan's typical crude humor building on Amy's reference, though a bit cheap

jokes.text: Loyalty? Money. Can't you just do this for Selina? No.
jokes.setup: Gary asks why Dan will help
jokes.punchline: Immediate money-focused response, then flat refusal to do it for loyalty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan character moment - his mercenary nature cuts through Gary's sentimentality

jokes.text: Nobody shakes hands in a craft shop.
jokes.setup: Dan and Amy shake hands after making deal
jokes.punchline: Gary's oddly specific knowledge of craft shop etiquette
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurd observation that feels completely Gary-like

jokes.text: You know, at least three of these kids are probably mine.
jokes.setup: Looking around at children in craft shop
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of promiscuity with disturbing mathematical precision
jokes.explanation: Classic Dan - casually dropping shocking personal information with no shame

jokes.text: No, no, let me get that, you sweet little invalid.
jokes.setup: Selina reaches for tissues while sick
jokes.punchline: Gary's condescending baby talk to the President
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary mothering behavior toward Selina, crossing professional boundaries

jokes.text: God, Gary, I wish to Jesus that you had gotten this flu instead of me. Oh, my God, me, too. Me, too.
jokes.setup: Selina wishes Gary was sick instead
jokes.punchline: Gary enthusiastically agrees he should be suffering instead of her
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of their toxic codependent relationship - Gary's eager self-sacrifice

jokes.text: And that is a Gary Walsh executive order.
jokes.setup: Gary insisting Selina needs to nap
jokes.punchline: Personal assistant declaring 'executive orders'
jokes.explanation: Gary's delusions of authority while still just being a bag carrier

jokes.text: 211 are voting against the bill. Five undecided. And we've got three yeses that we're working on turning around into nos. So defeat is within our grasp.
jokes.setup: Ben giving vote count update
jokes.punchline: 'Defeat is within our grasp' - celebrating losing their own bill
jokes.explanation: Perfect political satire - the upside-down logic of wanting to lose to win

jokes.text: There's also a cool tile game on here if you want to play a game. A what? A tile? Which, of course, you don't want to play.
jokes.setup: Gary offering iPad with voice memo app
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly suggesting games then immediately backtracking
jokes.explanation: Gary's nervous overexplaining and immediate retreat from suggestions

jokes.text: What I don't know, I can't lie about. Including this, whatever this is.
jokes.setup: Mike explaining his plausible deniability strategy
jokes.punchline: Immediately compromises it by acknowledging 'this, whatever this is'
jokes.explanation: Mike's self-defeating logic - trying to maintain deniability while acknowledging awareness

jokes.text: We're asking people to vote yes. We're doing what we should be doing. We're just doing it badly.
jokes.setup: Explaining how they're handling the conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Admitting they're intentionally doing their jobs badly
jokes.explanation: Perfect political doublespeak - maintaining appearances while sabotaging

jokes.text: I think I just sneezed up part of my pancreas.
jokes.setup: Selina sneezing while sick
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific internal organ reference
jokes.explanation: Selina's colorful exaggeration of illness symptoms

jokes.text: Don't worry, Mike. We have a team of our worst people on this. Cazzelli, Lennox. The usual saps. Jonah, Richard.
jokes.setup: Reassuring Mike about the operation
jokes.punchline: Admitting they deliberately chose incompetent people
jokes.explanation: Ben's brutal honesty about using incompetent staff as assets for failure

jokes.text: I didn't hear any of this because the acoustics are so bad.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to maintain plausible deniability
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse about room acoustics
jokes.explanation: Mike's pathetic attempt at maintaining deniability with a weak excuse

jokes.text: How did they write this thing? Get drunk and tape crayons to their feet?
jokes.setup: Congressman criticizing the bill's poor writing
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific image of drunk crayon-footed writing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd metaphor for incompetent legislation writing - highly quotable

jokes.text: Two giant children in their dads' suits.
jokes.setup: Congressman dismissing Jonah and Richard
jokes.punchline: Perfect visual description of their incompetence and youth
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical description that captures their essential incompetence and immaturity

jokes.text: We've glanced at the numbers, sir, and at first glance they are very compelling.
jokes.setup: Asked what the numbers say
jokes.punchline: Admits they only 'glanced' while claiming they're 'compelling'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - trying to sound professional while admitting complete incompetence

jokes.text: You don't know where the door is. I'll show you. It's over here.
jokes.setup: Kicking them out, then realizing they work for President
jokes.punchline: Helpful direction to the door as ultimate insult
jokes.explanation: Elegant burn that starts insulting their competence, then gets worse

jokes.text: So if I can offer any web design or support... Sir, can I ask you how you're going to vote today? No. Is that no as in I can't ask you or no as in...
jokes.setup: Richard offering random web services then asking for vote
jokes.punchline: Confusion over what the 'no' refers to
jokes.explanation: Richard's typical confusion and misdirection, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: You know, you just started that last sentence with a 'we' and you finished it with a 'you.'
jokes.setup: Ben asking Tom to stop the bill
jokes.punchline: Calling out the rhetorical shift from shared to individual responsibility
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political observation about how responsibility gets shifted in conversations

jokes.text: There is nothing like a Boston wedding. Let me tell you that. Wow, that was worryingly good, Tom. I mean, that was some black belt jujitsu bullshit right there.
jokes.setup: Expecting political discussion
jokes.punchline: Tom launches into wedding story, Ben impressed by deflection skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and Ben's genuine admiration for Tom's deflection skills

jokes.text: When was yours sent? When was yours sent? I asked you first.
jokes.setup: Gary and Kent both have conflicting memos about seeing Selina
jokes.punchline: Childish standoff about who asked first
jokes.explanation: Gary's petulant behavior when his authority is challenged

jokes.text: Yeah, ahem, or maybe I can get a new doctor. In which case I'll just say keep taking the fluids, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Doctor telling Selina to rest
jokes.punchline: Threat to fire him makes him immediately change medical advice
jokes.explanation: Doctor's immediate capitulation to presidential pressure, abandoning medical advice

jokes.text: I feel like I'm captain of the Titanic and I carved out the iceberg myself.
jokes.setup: Selina reflecting on sabotaging her own bill
jokes.punchline: Perfect metaphor for self-destruction with historical reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures the absurdity of their situation - highly quotable

jokes.text: I can sleep when I'm dead in about an hour.
jokes.setup: Gary insisting she needs rest
jokes.punchline: Specific timeline for her own death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with specific timing that makes it funnier than generic death reference

jokes.text: Not since Herod has there been a piece of legislation so damaging to average families.
jokes.setup: Dan arguing against the Families First Bill
jokes.punchline: Comparing modern legislation to biblical infanticide
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference that's both absurdly hyperbolic and politically sharp

jokes.text: This is akin to carrying out heart surgery with a pair of spoons.
jokes.setup: Continuing criticism of the bill
jokes.punchline: Absurd medical metaphor for legislative incompetence
jokes.explanation: Vivid absurd metaphor that effectively communicates incompetence

jokes.text: I don't need to be ridden by you two lobby horses to know that this bill is a waste of bowel space.
jokes.setup: Congressman responding to Dan and Amy's lobbying
jokes.punchline: 'Ridden by lobby horses' + 'waste of bowel space' combo
jokes.explanation: Creative horseback metaphor combined with scatological insult

jokes.text: And spoiler alert, they do not add up.
jokes.setup: Dan leaving numbers with congressman
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference to reveal the numbers are bad
jokes.explanation: Decent use of spoiler alert format, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Six-foot waxwork and his cuddly boyfriend?
jokes.setup: Asking about Jonah Ryan
jokes.punchline: Perfect physical description of Jonah and Richard
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual description - 'waxwork' captures Jonah's artificial quality perfectly

jokes.text: Call the 'Washington Post.' I smell conspiracy.
jokes.setup: Discovering both sides have same folder
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic conspiracy declaration over office supplies
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm about treating mundane details as major conspiracies

jokes.text: Okay, sure, so why is this one shaking like Elvis's leg?
jokes.setup: Noticing Dan is nervous
jokes.punchline: Perfect Elvis reference for nervous shaking
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference that perfectly describes nervous shaking

jokes.text: The president is qualified to be president because she is president. Does your party have a candidate who is president?
jokes.setup: Question about president's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Circular logic followed by impossible challenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect political doublespeak - circular logic that actually works rhetorically

jokes.text: Oh, no, I was just sending a memo. I didn't want to keep interrupting all the time. That is very considerate. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Selina talking during interview
jokes.punchline: Claims to be avoiding interruption while actively interrupting
jokes.explanation: Selina's complete lack of self-awareness about her own disruptive behavior

jokes.text: Send memo. Jesus. I'm not getting any memos. Oh, now I am.
jokes.setup: Selina using voice memo app during interview
jokes.punchline: Tech confusion creating awkward delay in receiving her own memos
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of Selina's tech incompetence during important moments

jokes.text: And you've got to be aggressive, though, you know? Man up here a little bit. Be aggressive? I'm starting to get really angry, Senator.
jokes.setup: Selina coaching Tom to be more aggressive
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate, over-the-top angry response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Tom taking direction too literally and immediately

jokes.text: That's it. I'm throwing the debate. You win, pretend opponent.
jokes.setup: Tom getting bad news about vote during practice debate
jokes.punchline: Giving up and addressing imaginary opponent
jokes.explanation: Good breaking of fourth wall within the scene - acknowledging the artifice

jokes.text: Oh, one. So one's okay. We just let off one nuke at the Super Bowl.
jokes.setup: Learning they sent people to see same congressman
jokes.punchline: Absurd nuclear Super Bowl metaphor for minor screw-up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben escalation - turning minor mistake into apocalyptic metaphor

jokes.text: I didn't hear any of this 'cause I have a really bad case of tinnitus, especially when discussing morally dubious things. So it's kind of like sinnitus.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to maintain plausible deniability
jokes.punchline: Clever portmanteau of 'sin' and 'tinnitus'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's both clever and perfectly fits Mike's character

jokes.text: But I'm implicated. I'm the linchpin. I could go to jail!
jokes.setup: Gary realizing he's involved in conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Dramatic self-importance about being the 'linchpin'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary - making himself the center of every crisis

jokes.text: This isn't about you, Gary. Jesus, nothing here is ever about you.
jokes.setup: Gary making everything about himself
jokes.punchline: Ben's brutal observation about Gary's irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben putdown that cuts to the heart of Gary's delusions

jokes.text: Listen, Sue, I'm involved with some stuff here. Scary stuff like take it to your grave kind of stuff. And that's the... that's the way I like it.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to sound important to Sue
jokes.punchline: Adds 'that's the way I like it' as if he enjoys mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Gary's attempt to seem dangerous and mysterious falls flat perfectly

jokes.text: I'm kind of having fun. I like having a cover story. Makes me feel like a Charlie's Angel.
jokes.setup: Dan asking why Amy isn't freaking out about the conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Amy enjoying the spy game with 70s reference
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Amy's unexpected playful side

jokes.text: Jesus, you know, I always thought the old Amy was kind of an uptight bitch, but now I sort of miss her.
jokes.setup: Amy being uncharacteristically relaxed
jokes.punchline: Dan preferring her when she was uptight
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Dan misses Amy's neurotic control because it's predictable

jokes.text: What, you mean not funny?
jokes.setup: Someone says situation is like political cartoon
jokes.punchline: Ben's dry observation about political cartoon quality
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about political cartoons generally not being funny

jokes.text: No, mine was the master list. Your list was a sub-list. It was a sur-list, which means over-list.
jokes.setup: Argument about whose list has authority
jokes.punchline: Kent's made-up etymology of 'sur-list'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent - pedantic wordplay to establish bureaucratic superiority

jokes.text: Your earwax could bring down a president.
jokes.setup: Ben angry at Kent for the mixup
jokes.punchline: Absurd imagery of earwax as presidential threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd insult connecting personal hygiene to political consequences

jokes.text: And then we're gonna have to destroy the evidence and that's conspiracy to commit fraud. No, a cash trail is very fucking bad for us.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining legal consequences
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact discussion of destroying evidence
jokes.explanation: Ben's casual approach to serious crimes, treating it like logistics

jokes.text: There, you've been blooded. So join the circle jerk. Grab a dick.
jokes.setup: Ben involving Mike in the conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Mixing military initiation with sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective metaphor for unwilling inclusion in conspiracy

jokes.text: Wish I could hear what you were saying over that incessant drilling sound.
jokes.setup: Mike being told about conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Made-up excuse about drilling to maintain deniability
jokes.explanation: Mike's increasingly desperate excuses for not hearing incriminating information

jokes.text: But you're not. Oh, my God, now you are! Well done. Now I feel a lot better.
jokes.setup: Ben telling Mike he's not implicated
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal after Mike learns details, plus satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Ben's relief at dragging someone else down with him

jokes.text: My future depends on that dumb fucking elf?
jokes.setup: Learning Pierce is the deciding vote
jokes.punchline: Perfect physical description of Pierce
jokes.explanation: 'Dumb fucking elf' is a perfect description that's both visual and character-based

jokes.text: And your support will shrink faster than a penis in the December Hudson.
jokes.setup: Dan threatening Pierce about bill consequences
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor combining cold weather and anatomy
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific metaphor that's both geographically and seasonally precise

jokes.text: You shit-eating cocksuckers.
jokes.setup: Jonah and Richard arriving at hospital
jokes.punchline: Amy's immediate profanity barrage
jokes.explanation: Generic profanity without much craft, though fits Amy's frustration

jokes.text: That's exactly what you just did, dumbass.
jokes.setup: Amy accusing them of strong-arming Pierce
jokes.punchline: Dan pointing out her hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Good comeback pointing out Amy's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: She's probably gonna die just to spite me. That evil bitch.
jokes.setup: Learning Pierce's mother might be dying
jokes.punchline: Making the woman's potential death about herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina narcissism - even stranger's death is personal attack

jokes.text: Well, this is a long glass of frozen strawberry fuckup.
jokes.setup: Assessing the situation at hospital
jokes.punchline: Creative cocktail metaphor for disaster
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor mixing bar drink with disaster - very quotable

jokes.text: I don't want to be a bagman for the toughest guy in D block.
jokes.setup: Gary worried about going to prison
jokes.punchline: Specific prison scenario with hierarchy reference
jokes.explanation: Gary's specific prison fears show his neurotic imagination

jokes.text: You shouldn't even be here, Jonah, unless you're giving your brain to science.
jokes.setup: Amy confronting Jonah at hospital
jokes.punchline: Suggests he donate his defective brain
jokes.explanation: Clever insult that ties hospital setting to Jonah's stupidity

jokes.text: Do you remember her? She's the woman that would have fired you if you hadn't gone fucking Fukushima.
jokes.setup: Amy reminding Jonah about the president
jokes.punchline: Nuclear disaster metaphor for Jonah's meltdown/departure
jokes.explanation: Perfect metaphor for Jonah's career implosion using nuclear disaster

jokes.text: I am surprised the two of you even found your way out of your own mothers' vaginas. Oh, my God, dude, I got out of there like I had a fucking map.
jokes.setup: Amy's crude insult about basic competence
jokes.punchline: Jonah's enthusiastic, literal response to birth metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - missing the insult completely and responding with bizarre pride

jokes.text: Do you think I want to be in this fucking Minecraft piece of shit?
jokes.setup: Dan forced to ride in Richard's car
jokes.punchline: Minecraft reference for blocky, cheap car design
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor connecting car design to video game aesthetics

jokes.text: You colossal fucking fanny pack.
jokes.setup: Dan insulting Jonah
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to outdated, ridiculous accessory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult comparing Jonah to something both large and embarrassing

jokes.text: You're so stupid, you don't even know that you're being used for your stupidity.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining Jonah's role
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about being unaware of awareness
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-insult about Jonah's recursive stupidity - very quotable

jokes.text: I took a precision driving class, Dan. I think I know what I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Richard backing up instead of going forward
jokes.punchline: Claims expertise while demonstrating incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - confidence inversely related to competence

jokes.text: We should have hired Navy SEALs instead of those two actual seals.
jokes.setup: Learning they lost Pierce
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jonah and Richard to marine mammals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels - military elite vs. useless animals

jokes.text: How do you lose a whole person? A cuff link I get, but not a person.
jokes.setup: Disbelief about losing Pierce
jokes.punchline: Scale comparison from small item to human being
jokes.explanation: Good scale joke that emphasizes the absurdity through comparison

jokes.text: You guys are. You're parasites. You're like an infestation of mediocrity.
jokes.setup: Selina angry at her staff
jokes.punchline: Mixing biological and quality metaphors
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor combining pest control with performance criticism

jokes.text: You're Fozzie Bear who's been ripped up and used to smuggle heroin.
jokes.setup: Selina insulting Mike
jokes.punchline: Muppet reference combined with drug trafficking
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant surreal insult combining childhood innocence with criminal activity

jokes.text: Call NASA. They've got to have a satellite that can pinpoint a moron.
jokes.setup: Selina ordering staff to find Pierce
jokes.punchline: Suggesting NASA technology for stupidity detection
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation using space technology for mundane incompetence tracking

jokes.text: If you want to catch a fugitive, you got to think like a fugitive. Where's he going? Uh, to the airport. Why would he be going to the airport? To catch a plane, Dan.
jokes.setup: Jonah's attempt at detective reasoning
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that goes nowhere useful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah logic - sounds smart but provides no actual insight

jokes.text: That's the kind of shit that comes back to bite you when you run for office. Yeah, that's what's gonna hold you back.
jokes.setup: Jonah worried about getting traffic ticket
jokes.punchline: Dan's sarcastic observation about Jonah's real obstacles
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm about Jonah's delusions regarding his political viability

jokes.text: Okay, new rule in the Cube. If your name begins with D, you need to shut the fuck up immediately. I'm fine because my name begins with an R.
jokes.setup: Richard making arbitrary car rules
jokes.punchline: Self-serving alphabetical exemption for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard - creating arbitrary authority while exempting himself

jokes.text: Not if we shorten it to Dick. Well, I mean...
jokes.setup: Dan pointing out Richard's nickname starts with D
jokes.punchline: Richard's confused realization his rule backfires
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his arbitrary rule immediately undermining him

jokes.text: But she might get better. Or she might not get better. She could be buried with a book.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining his mother's condition
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non sequitur about burial with reading material
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce tangent - starting serious then wandering into absurdity

jokes.text: I feel like I'm about to kick it old school and die in office.
jokes.setup: Selina feeling terrible while sick
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to presidential death with slang
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor mixing casual slang with presidential mortality

jokes.text: Guess I should kill it. Is the right answer.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if killing the bill is right
jokes.punchline: Answering her own rhetorical question
jokes.explanation: Selina's self-justification and immediate validation of her decision

jokes.text: You could grow tomatoes in here.
jokes.setup: Debate about room temperature
jokes.punchline: Agricultural metaphor for excessive heat
jokes.explanation: Decent hyperbole for hot room, though not particularly original

jokes.text: You can't come in. That comes from the president. Well, she's a bitch.
jokes.setup: Tom blocking Amy from White House
jokes.punchline: Amy's immediate, casual insult of president
jokes.explanation: Amy's unfiltered response to authority, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: See you later, ghosts of staffers past.
jokes.setup: Mike saying goodbye to Dan and Amy
jokes.punchline: Christmas Carol reference to former colleagues
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that captures their exile from the administration

jokes.text: I brought him also, ma'am. Richard Splett. She already knows who you are. You can go. No, no, no. Not you. Not you.
jokes.setup: Richard trying to get credit for bringing Pierce
jokes.punchline: Gary's correction about who's being dismissed
jokes.explanation: Richard's attention-seeking and Gary's clarification creates mild confusion comedy

jokes.text: Dallas. I love Dallas, Texas. No, it needs to be a foreign city.
jokes.setup: Asked for favorite city for ambassadorship
jokes.punchline: Pierce's confusion about domestic vs. foreign cities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - fundamental misunderstanding of ambassadorial role

jokes.text: J'adore Paris. I've never been, but it looks amazing.
jokes.setup: Pierce suggesting Paris for ambassadorship
jokes.punchline: Admits he's never been to the city he 'adores'
jokes.explanation: Pierce's superficial knowledge presented as expertise

jokes.text: Okay, the president, she wants me to vote against her bill? Am I being stupid here? Yes. Yes. No. I mean, you're... No, you're not being...
jokes.setup: Pierce confused about voting against president's bill
jokes.punchline: Gary's confused attempt to explain without explaining
jokes.explanation: Good confusion comedy with Gary's inability to give clear instructions

jokes.text: You can have Paris. What? I can have Paris? Wow, thank you.
jokes.setup: Gary making executive decision about ambassadorship
jokes.punchline: Pierce's excitement about Gary's unauthorized promise
jokes.explanation: Gary overstepping authority with major consequences, Pierce's naive gratitude

jokes.text: For a moment, Gary was the most powerful person in the world.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on Gary's unauthorized ambassadorship promise
jokes.punchline: Describing Gary's brief, accidental authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect observation about how incompetence briefly became ultimate power

jokes.text: We'll call him after the vote, tell him it's Mogadishu.
jokes.setup: Gary accidentally promising Pierce Paris instead of somewhere terrible
jokes.punchline: Somalia as the reality vs Paris as the promise
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about diplomatic punishment posting contrasted with Pierce's romantic expectations

jokes.text: He even walks dumb. Every time I see him, I hear circus music.
jokes.setup: Watching Pierce on TV vote
jokes.punchline: Associating his movement with clown music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy - describing Pierce's physical presence as inherently comedic

jokes.text: Come on, Owen. It's your time to shine, you dim fucking bulb.
jokes.setup: Encouraging Pierce to vote
jokes.punchline: Light metaphors that contradict each other
jokes.explanation: Perfect wordplay using opposing light metaphors to capture the contradiction

jokes.text: Apart from, like, having a raccoon take a shit in my head.
jokes.setup: Selina describing how she feels physically
jokes.punchline: Vivid, specific animal metaphor for illness
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disgusting metaphor for feeling sick

jokes.text: Look, I wouldn't ask you to do anything I wouldn't do myself. And while that isn't true, find some comfort in it.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to comfort Mike about lying
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits the comfort is false
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - offering false comfort while admitting it's false in the same breath

jokes.text: She sounds like an underwater Bob Dylan.
jokes.setup: Selina giving speech with hoarse voice
jokes.punchline: Specific musical reference with aquatic twist
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor combining Dylan's already unclear voice with water distortion

jokes.text: Fuck you, America.
jokes.setup: Selina alone after difficult day
jokes.punchline: Direct insult to entire country she leads
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Selina's frustration - president insulting her constituents

jokes.text: That's why I included the second glossory.
jokes.setup: Voice memo about not understanding Kent's document
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'glossary' while explaining glossary
jokes.explanation: Good Selina moment - mispronouncing the very word that's supposed to help pronunciation

jokes.text: How do you leave a memo in your sleep?
jokes.setup: Mike discovering sleep-memo from Selina
jokes.punchline: Logical question about unconscious technology use
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to voice memo running gag, though not particularly strong
